This field consists of the borough code followed by the tax block followed by the tax lot. The borough code is one numeric digit. The tax block is one to five numeric digits, preceded with leading zeros when the block is less than five digits. The tax lot is one to four digits and is preceded with leading zeros when the lot is less than four digits. For condominiums, the BBL is for the billing lot. See TAX LOT for more information on how condominiums are handled. Examples: Manhattan Borough Code 1, Tax Block 16, Tax Lot 100 would be stored as 1000160100.
